nativeRange.habitat: Shallow rocky and reef substrates in well-lit, high-flow zones within the photic zone

avoidWith: heavy herbivores (tangs, rabbitfish) in small tanks that may graze macroalgae

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Ghastly Liagora / Knobby Liagora (Liagora sp.). Sole hobby-trade source for this entry.
Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Purple Iridescent Liagora (Liagora sp.), a color variant consolidated into this entry.

summary: A lightly calcified branching red macroalga (family Liagoraceae) sold under several Mosaic Macros trade names. Features delicate, slippery white-to-purple branches and benefits from high water flow in display reef tanks.
careNotes: Ghastly Liagora (Liagora sp.) is a specialty ornamental red macroalga in the family Liagoraceae, order Nemaliales — the same family and order as the broader Liagora genus, which encompasses dozens of species distributed across tropical and warm-temperate oceans worldwide (Indo-Pacific, tropical Atlantic, and Mediterranean). The genus is lightly calcified, depositing some calcium carbonate in the thallus, though not to the extent of heavily calcareous genera such as Halimeda or Penicillus; this entry accordingly uses the general 'macroalgae' form designation. Mosaic Macros sells this species under two trade names consolidated here: 'Ghastly Liagora' (a white/purple branching form, sometimes called 'Knobby Liagora') and 'Purple Iridescent Liagora' (a purple form with iridescent highlights along the thallus). Both feature spindly, slippery, distinctively branching thalli. Mosaic Macros is the sole known hobby-trade source for this genus at the time of writing; species-specific captive care data is not available in the broader aquarium hobby literature. Standard reef water parameters are generally advised: temperature around 24–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, salinity SG 1.023–1.026. High water flow is noted as important on the Mosaic product page. Husbandry values for lighting (medium), growth rate (slow), and nutrient uptake (low) are extrapolated from published care standards for ornamental red macroalgae in Nemaliales and from general Liagoraceae biology. Notably, Mosaic Macros flags this species as 'VERY sensitive to shipping,' requiring next-day air for the live arrival guarantee — this sensitivity likely carries over to aquarium acclimation and water quality stability, reinforcing an intermediate care level. Propagation in the aquarium is generally by fragmentation. This entry is based on Mosaic Macros product pages plus family-level taxonomic and care extrapolation; a reviewer should verify captive care parameters when additional hobby-level documentation becomes available. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is generally accomplished by fragmentation — detaching healthy branch segments and securing them to rockwork or rubble. Sexual reproduction (the alternating gametophyte–sporophyte life cycle typical of Rhodophyta) has not been documented in hobby aquaria for this genus.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Primary hobby-trade source. Provides species identification, imagery, high-flow requirement note, and shipping sensitivity data. No numeric water parameters given; standard reef parameters applied from family-level extrapolation.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Purple Iridescent Liagora
additional.notes: Second Mosaic Macros listing consolidated into this entry; describes a purple iridescent color form of Liagora sp. Confirms high-flow requirement and shipping sensitivity consistent with the primary listing.

additional.name: GBIF — Liagora J.V.F.Lamouroux, 1812 (taxonomy)
additional.notes: Used for taxonomic confirmation of genus placement in Liagoraceae / Nemaliales and broad distribution data (tropical and warm-temperate Atlantic, Indian, and Pacific Oceans).

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
